claim: Autologous transplantation of mesenchymal stem cells has lower rates of rejection than induction therapy with anti-interleukin-2 receptor antibodies.

result.claim: Autologous transplantation of mesenchymal stem cells has lower rates of rejection than induction therapy with anti-interleukin-2 receptor antibodies.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from a randomized controlled trial (Paper ID: 10582939). This study directly compared autologous mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) transplantation with anti-interleukin-2 receptor antibody induction therapy in living-related kidney transplant recipients. The results showed significantly lower rates of biopsy-confirmed acute rejection in the autologous MSC groups. Specifically, after 6 months, the acute rejection rates were 7.5% (4 of 53 patients) in the autologous MSC plus standard-dose CNI group (prop_1051) and 7.7% (4 of 52 patients) in the low-dose MSC group (prop_1052), compared to 21.6% (11 of 51 controls) in the anti-IL-2 receptor antibody control group (prop_1053). Furthermore, none of the MSC-treated patients experienced glucocorticoid-resistant rejection, while 7.8% (4 patients) in the control group did (prop_1054, prop_1055). The abstract of the paper explicitly concludes that autologous MSCs resulted in a lower incidence of acute rejection compared to anti-IL-2 receptor antibody induction therapy.

evidence.text: Autospecific CD4(+) T cells showed increased Th2 cytokine responses in the absence of IL-12.
evidence.source: Dual role of the IL-12/IFN-gamma axis in the development of autoimmune myocarditis: induction by IL-12 and protection by IFN-gamma.

evidence.text: After 6 months, 11 of 51 controls (21.6%) had biopsy-confirmed acute rejection.

evidence.text: The presence of an autoimmune disease-associated IL2RA haplotype correlates with diminished IL-2 responsiveness in Ag-experienced CD4(+) T cells, as measured by phosphorylation of STAT5a.
evidence.source: Type 1 diabetes-associated IL2RA variation lowers IL-2 signaling and contributes to diminished CD4+CD25+ regulatory T cell function.

evidence.text: Patients were inoculated with marrow-derived autologous MSC (1-2 x 10(6)/kg) at kidney reperfusion and two weeks later.
evidence.source: Induction therapy with autologous mesenchymal stem cells in living-related kidney transplants: a randomized controlled trial.

evidence.text: CD4(+)CD25(+) regulatory T cells (Tregs) from individuals with a range of human autoimmune diseases are deficient in their ability to control autologous proinflammatory responses when compared with nondiseased, control individuals.
